metadata.packName: Banking and Finance Microsoft 365 E5 Technologies and Activities
metadata.licenseAssumption: Microsoft 365 E5 with Microsoft Purview, Microsoft Defender, Microsoft Entra ID P2, Intune, Power BI, and Copilot for Microsoft 365 where assigned
metadata.purpose: Provide Codex and browser-based agents with a technology and activity catalog for realistic daily, weekly, monthly, and event-driven banking/finance simulations.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Departmental and cross-functional document collaboration for loan packages, KYC reviews, audit evidence, policy documents, regulatory response files, and executive committee packs.
commonDailyActivities: Create or update customer review files in controlled document libraries.
Edit Excel workbooks containing fictional account exposure or case queues.
Upload KYC packets or customer dispute evidence to department sites.
Review version history for regulatory or audit response documents.
Apply or update sensitivity labels on sensitive financial documents.
commonWeeklyActivities: Update fraud review and AML investigation folders.
Share risk committee documents with restricted groups.
Review access permissions on sensitive workspaces.
commonMonthlyActivities: Publish compliance attestations and control evidence.
Review retention and records classification for regulated files.
eventDrivenActivities: Create a restricted workspace for a regulatory request.
Break permission inheritance after oversharing is detected.
Revoke anonymous or broad sharing links.
Move files from a broad collaboration site to a restricted legal or security site.
Apply a higher sensitivity label after sensitive data is detected.
normalBehaviorExamples: Carlos updates a weekly credit-risk workbook in a restricted Risk Analytics library.
Emily uploads a regulator response draft to a Legal Privileged site.
Sofia updates a project RAID log in a PMO SharePoint site.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon uploads a KYC workbook to a broad Operations site instead of the restricted Banking Operations site.
A user creates an organization-wide sharing link for a loan review package.
A confidential AML workbook is copied into a project site with inherited permissions.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Personal work staging area for drafts, analysis files, exports, meeting notes, and working copies before formal publication to SharePoint.
commonDailyActivities: Sync working copies of Excel files from SharePoint.
Download Power BI or Excel exports for local analysis.
Share a draft with one colleague for review.
Rename or duplicate files for versioning.
commonWeeklyActivities: Stage loan committee drafts before publishing.
commonMonthlyActivities: Create month-end finance close working files.
Archive personal working drafts to official repositories.
Review local synced folders for sensitive data.
eventDrivenActivities: A user downloads a large volume of customer files after a role-change notice.
A sensitive file is shared from OneDrive to an external vendor.
A draft containing account-like values is uploaded from a local device.
A sensitive file is copied from OneDrive sync to a network share.
A user attempts to share a file externally and triggers DLP.
normalBehaviorExamples: Priya stores a temporary feature-analysis workbook before publishing an approved aggregate to SharePoint.
Diego drafts a customer-safe proposal and shares it with Emily for legal review.
Laura stores a personal draft of a workforce planning summary before uploading the final HR version.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon downloads multiple restricted files to OneDrive and creates an external sharing link.
A finance analyst syncs a treasury workbook to an unmanaged endpoint.
A user shares a local draft containing customer account-like numbers with a personal mailbox.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Collaboration hub for operations, risk committees, customer escalations, legal reviews, fraud response, and executive coordination.
commonDailyActivities: Discuss customer operations cases in department channels.
Ask whether documents can be sent to external parties.
Coordinate urgent review of customer disputes or fraud alerts.
Discuss DLP or label warnings with security teams.
commonWeeklyActivities: Run weekly risk committee chats and meetings.
eventDrivenActivities: Escalate a suspected data leak.
Ask security whether an external share is allowed.
Share the wrong file in a channel.
Discuss a customer complaint involving sensitive details.
Coordinate incident containment after risky file activity.
normalBehaviorExamples: Sofia posts the weekly PMO status link to the project team channel.
Marcus posts a reminder about external sharing controls.
David asks for review of a customer complaint summary before sending it externally.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon posts a KYC spreadsheet directly in a broad Teams channel.
A user pastes account-like numbers into a Teams chat.
A Teams channel includes a guest user while internal risk notes are being discussed.
primaryPurviewControls: Sensitivity labels for Teams and groups

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Formal internal and external communication for customer notices, vendor coordination, legal correspondence, audit requests, finance approvals, and executive reporting.
commonWeeklyActivities: Coordinate legal review of contract or regulatory drafts.
commonMonthlyActivities: Send payroll and compensation approval notices.
eventDrivenActivities: Send an email to an external domain with a sensitive attachment.
Reply-all with internal risk commentary.
Forward a customer complaint containing account-like values.
Trigger DLP warning for financial data.
Attempt to override a DLP block with justification.
normalBehaviorExamples: Emily emails an approved clean legal redline to external counsel.
James sends a monthly operations summary to leadership.
Diego sends a customer-safe proposal PDF to a fictional customer.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon attaches an internal KYC workbook to an external vendor email.
A finance analyst sends a treasury worksheet with bank-like values to a broad distribution list.
A support user forwards a customer dispute thread with internal notes to an external party.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Browser-based editing of Word, Excel, and PowerPoint files for customer analysis, risk workbooks, executive decks, and policy documents.
commonDailyActivities: Edit customer account analysis workbooks in Excel Web.
Update policy drafts in Word Web.
Prepare executive and committee decks in PowerPoint Web.
Apply sensitivity labels during save or close.
Use comments and mentions to request review.
commonWeeklyActivities: Add comments to legal and compliance drafts.
commonMonthlyActivities: Update HR compensation or workforce planning documents.
eventDrivenActivities: A label recommendation appears after sensitive values are added.
A user downgrades a label to share externally.
A user removes a label from a sanitized derivative file.
A file is edited after being shared externally.
Comments reveal sensitive details not present in the main document body.
normalBehaviorExamples: Carlos edits a credit exposure workbook and applies Confidential.
Emily updates a legal response draft and applies Legal Privileged.
Sofia edits a PMO deck and removes internal risk notes before export.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon removes a Confidential label from a customer account workbook before sharing it.
A user pastes account-like values into comments that are later shared externally.
A sensitive workbook is exported to PDF without preserving the intended label.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: AI-assisted summarization, drafting, analysis, meeting recap, file discovery, and executive briefing creation over permitted Microsoft 365 content.
commonDailyActivities: Find relevant policy or procedure documents.
Draft internal status updates from source files.
eventDrivenActivities: Copilot surfaces sensitive files due to broad permissions.
A user prompts Copilot to summarize a confidential customer file.
AI output inherits sensitive information from source documents.
A user asks Copilot to create external content from internal-only sources.
Copilot becomes part of an insider risk narrative after sensitive file discovery.
normalBehaviorExamples: Alexander asks Copilot to summarize approved executive briefing files.
Diego asks Copilot to draft a customer-safe follow-up from sanitized proposal notes.
Priya asks Copilot to summarize aggregated model trends.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon asks Copilot to summarize KYC files he should not need for his role.
A user asks Copilot for all documents containing account-like numbers.
A user generates an external response from internal fraud investigation notes.
primaryPurviewControls: Data Security Posture Management for AI

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Classification, labeling, protection, and encryption of regulated financial, legal, HR, and customer data.
commonDailyActivities: Apply labels to new customer files.
Create customer-safe derivatives with lower labels.
commonWeeklyActivities: Review files missing labels in sensitive workspaces.
Validate label coverage for loan committee materials.
Check label analytics for finance and HR content.
commonMonthlyActivities: Validate regulated financial data label adoption.
Review label-based access and encryption behavior.
Report label coverage to governance committee.
eventDrivenActivities: A user removes a label before external sharing.
Auto-labeling recommends Highly Confidential for an AML workbook.
A protected file is accessed by an unexpected user.
A label downgrade requires business justification.
A file is relabeled after legal or HR content is added.
normalBehaviorExamples: Emily labels regulator response drafts as Legal Privileged.
Laura labels compensation planning files as HR Restricted.
Carlos labels credit-risk workbooks as Regulated Financial Data.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon removes a Highly Confidential label before sending a file externally.
A user applies Public to a workbook containing customer account-like numbers.
A protected file is copied into a location where label enforcement is weaker.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Prevent or monitor unsafe sharing of customer account data, financial identifiers, payment-like references, HR compensation data, legal content, and regulated banking records.
commonDailyActivities: Warn users before sending sensitive attachments externally.
Block emails containing regulated financial patterns.
Audit file sharing from SharePoint and OneDrive.
Detect customer account-like values in Teams messages.
Capture user justifications for policy overrides.
commonWeeklyActivities: Tune false positives for synthetic banking identifiers.
Analyze top risky users and locations.
commonMonthlyActivities: Report DLP incidents to governance committee.
Refine policies for new banking scenarios.
eventDrivenActivities: A file with customer account-like numbers is emailed externally.
A user attempts to copy a regulated workbook to USB.
A Teams message includes payment-like references.
A user overrides a warning to send a file to a vendor.
A large customer CSV export is downloaded and shared.
normalBehaviorExamples: Marcus reviews DLP alerts and closes expected test events.
Ana tunes DLP rules for financial identifiers and HR records.
Carlos receives a warning when attempting to share a sensitive workbook with a partner.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon overrides a DLP warning to send a KYC workbook externally.
A user copies an AML case export to a USB device.
A broad Teams channel receives a pasted list of customer account-like numbers.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Correlate risky user activities such as mass downloads, sensitive file access, label removal, external sharing, endpoint copy, resignation indicators, and unusual work patterns.
commonDailyActivities: Correlate DLP events with HR or role-change context.
Escalate cases to Security or HR.
commonWeeklyActivities: Assess risky behavior patterns by department.
Review repeated label removals or overrides.
Conduct case review with HR and Legal.
commonMonthlyActivities: Validate privacy and role-based access controls.
eventDrivenActivities: A user downloads many files after a performance or role-change event.
A user prints and copies confidential files to a network share.
A user accesses files outside normal role scope.
A user shares sensitive data externally after multiple DLP warnings.
A user removes labels from files before sharing.
normalBehaviorExamples: Marcus reviews a simulated case involving multiple sensitive downloads.
Ana validates whether Devon's actions were negligent or malicious.
Emily provides legal guidance on investigation boundaries.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon downloads AML case files, prints loan data, and uploads a CSV to an external app.
A user repeatedly removes labels from customer account files.
A user accesses legal privileged content unrelated to their role.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Monitor cloud app usage, detect risky sharing, identify unmanaged app uploads, control sessions, and investigate anomalous file activity.
commonDailyActivities: Detect downloads from sensitive SharePoint sites.
Review impossible travel or anomalous session activity.
Apply session controls for sensitive files.
commonWeeklyActivities: Review sanctioned and unsanctioned app usage.
commonMonthlyActivities: Coordinate with compliance for cloud risk posture.
eventDrivenActivities: A user uploads a customer CSV to an unmanaged AI app.
A user downloads a large number of sensitive files from SharePoint.
A risky session is blocked or monitored.
A sensitive file is shared through an unmanaged app.
A user accesses content from an unusual location.
normalBehaviorExamples: Ana reviews cloud app usage for approved financial analysis tools.
Marcus monitors risky sessions involving regulated financial data.
Miguel reviews app governance alerts for suspicious OAuth grants.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon uploads a customer dispute export to an unmanaged AI summarization site.
A user downloads regulated financial data from an unmanaged device.
An external app receives an exported Power BI customer report.

technologies.technologyName: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint and Intune-managed endpoints
technologies.businessUseInBanking: Protect and monitor managed devices used for financial analysis, customer operations, remote work, and secure access to sensitive banking files.
commonDailyActivities: Access SharePoint and OneDrive from managed devices.
Print customer or finance documents when allowed.
commonWeeklyActivities: Patch and update banking operations devices.
eventDrivenActivities: A sensitive file is copied to USB.
A regulated workbook is printed.
A file is copied to a network share.
A user accesses sensitive data from a non-compliant device.
A malware or suspicious process touches sensitive files.
normalBehaviorExamples: Carlos opens a confidential credit workbook on a compliant managed laptop.
Laura prints an approved HR summary after label policy allows printing.
James syncs approved operations files using OneDrive on a managed device.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon copies a KYC export to USB after a DLP warning.
A user prints a legal privileged document outside approved workflow.
A non-compliant device downloads a treasury workbook.

technologies.technologyName: Microsoft Entra ID and Conditional Access
technologies.businessUseInBanking: Identity security, access governance, MFA enforcement, device compliance, privileged access, and conditional access policies for sensitive banking workloads.
commonDailyActivities: Use MFA for privileged or risky sessions.
Access restricted sites based on user role.
Review sign-in risk for unusual access.
commonWeeklyActivities: Review guest access and external collaboration.
Review access packages and group membership changes.
commonMonthlyActivities: Run access reviews for sensitive banking groups.
eventDrivenActivities: A user accesses sensitive content from an unusual location.
A guest user is added to a restricted team.
A risky sign-in blocks access to SharePoint.
An access review removes broad permissions.
Privileged access is activated to investigate an incident.
normalBehaviorExamples: Ana reviews access to the Regulated Financial Data SharePoint group.
Marcus investigates a risky sign-in alert.
Miguel activates a privileged role to review a security configuration.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon signs in from an unusual location and downloads sensitive files.
A guest account receives access to a team containing KYC files.
A broad dynamic group grants access to HR or finance content.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Executive, risk, finance, branch operations, credit exposure, fraud trends, and customer operations reporting.
commonDailyActivities: Share dashboard links with internal teams.
commonWeeklyActivities: Review weekly risk and fraud dashboards.
commonMonthlyActivities: Generate monthly finance and risk reports.
eventDrivenActivities: A user exports customer-level credit-risk data.
A dashboard is shared with a broad group.
A report export is emailed externally.
A dataset refresh fails before a regulatory deadline.
A user downloads sensitive underlying data.
normalBehaviorExamples: Carlos views a credit exposure dashboard with role-based access.
Alexander reviews an executive finance dashboard.
Priya exports aggregated model trend data.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon exports underlying customer-level data from a report and emails it externally.
A user shares a Power BI report with a broad group containing account-level metrics.
A CSV export from Power BI is uploaded to an unmanaged AI tool.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Structured tracking of KYC cases, customer complaints, regulatory tasks, audit evidence, vendor due diligence, and operational approvals.
commonWeeklyActivities: Export list items for committee review.
eventDrivenActivities: A list export includes customer IDs and complaint details.
A user shares a list view externally.
A broad group receives edit access to a sensitive list.
A KYC case is escalated to Legal or Security.
A list attachment contains regulated data.
normalBehaviorExamples: David updates customer complaint statuses in a restricted list.
Sofia tracks regulatory response tasks in a PMO list.
Laura tracks HR onboarding exceptions in an HR list.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon exports a complaints list containing customer account-like identifiers.
A list with KYC notes is shared with an external guest.
A user attaches a sensitive spreadsheet to a list item with broad access.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Task management for audits, regulatory responses, customer remediation, loan committee preparation, incident response, and PMO execution.
commonDailyActivities: Assign owners for missing customer documents.
Track due dates for regulatory requests.
commonMonthlyActivities: Export task status for governance reporting.
eventDrivenActivities: A task includes a link to a sensitive file with broad access.
A user attaches confidential evidence to a broadly visible plan.
Incident response tasks reference sensitive investigation details.
A user exports task data with regulated references.
A guest user is added to a plan containing internal remediation notes.
normalBehaviorExamples: Sofia assigns audit evidence collection tasks to department owners.
Marcus creates incident response tasks after a DLP alert.
Emily tracks legal review tasks for a regulator response.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon attaches a customer account workbook to a broadly visible Planner task.
A task comment includes internal fraud investigation details.
A guest can view a plan containing regulatory response notes.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Collect internal attestations, customer feedback, control evidence responses, training acknowledgements, and incident intake details.
commonMonthlyActivities: Export regulator response readiness survey results.
eventDrivenActivities: A form captures customer account-like values in free text.
A form response export is shared externally.
A form is configured as public when it should be internal only.
Sensitive attachments are requested through an inappropriate form.
A user exports responses containing HR or customer data.
normalBehaviorExamples: Ana collects security control attestation responses.
David collects customer complaint intake details through an internal form.
Sofia collects project readiness confirmations.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon creates a public form asking for account-like customer identifiers.
A Forms export containing customer complaints is emailed externally.
A user collects HR details through a form with broad access.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Lightweight collaborative components for meeting notes, action items, customer escalation summaries, risk decisions, and project status updates.
commonDailyActivities: Create shared action items during Teams discussions.
Update meeting decisions in Loop components.
Copy Loop content into documents or emails.
commonMonthlyActivities: Archive key decisions into SharePoint documents.
Review sensitive content in collaborative notes.
eventDrivenActivities: A Loop component includes account-like identifiers.
A component is shared into a channel with guest users.
A user copies confidential investigation notes into Loop.
A Loop component is converted into an email sent externally.
Copilot summarizes Loop notes containing sensitive data.
normalBehaviorExamples: Sofia uses Loop to track PMO action items.
Marcus uses Loop for security remediation actions.
David uses Loop to coordinate internal customer complaint next steps.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon pastes KYC case details into a Loop component shared in a broad Teams channel.
A legal privileged discussion is summarized in a Loop note accessible to non-legal users.
A Loop component containing customer identifiers is copied into an external email.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Visual collaboration for process mapping, incident timelines, loan workflow design, fraud investigation brainstorming, and executive workshops.
commonMonthlyActivities: Review diagrams for sensitive content before sharing.
eventDrivenActivities: A whiteboard includes customer account-like values.
An incident timeline diagram is shared externally.
A process map includes internal control gaps.
A user exports a sensitive whiteboard as an image or PDF.
A guest user accesses an internal investigation whiteboard.
normalBehaviorExamples: Ana maps the security investigation workflow during a Teams meeting.
Sofia maps project dependencies for a steering committee.
James maps banking operations process improvements.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon adds customer account-like values to a whiteboard shared with a vendor.
An incident timeline whiteboard is exported and emailed externally.
A whiteboard includes internal fraud control weaknesses.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Meeting recordings, training videos, executive briefings, security awareness clips, compliance sessions, and operations walkthroughs stored in SharePoint/OneDrive.
commonDailyActivities: Share meeting recordings with internal teams.
commonMonthlyActivities: Review access to executive meeting recordings.
eventDrivenActivities: A recording transcript includes customer account-like values.
An executive meeting recording is shared broadly.
A training video includes internal control details.
A recording is downloaded after a sensitive meeting.
A guest user accesses a restricted meeting recording.
normalBehaviorExamples: Marcus publishes a security awareness recording to an internal training site.
Sofia shares a PMO meeting recording with the project team.
Ana reviews an incident response meeting transcript.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon downloads a compliance meeting recording that includes sensitive customer examples.
An executive meeting recording is shared with all employees.
A transcript containing account-like identifiers is exported to an external vendor.

technologies.businessUseInBanking: Internal community communication, announcements, training discussions, culture updates, and broad organizational engagement.
eventDrivenActivities: A user posts customer details in a public community.
A user shares a link to a sensitive file in a broad community.
A discussion reveals internal incident details.
A compliance post includes too much operational detail.
Communication Compliance flags risky wording.
normalBehaviorExamples: Marcus posts a monthly awareness reminder about external sharing.
James posts an operations excellence update.
Laura posts an HR training reminder without sensitive employee data.
riskyBehaviorExamples: Devon posts a question containing a customer account-like number in a broad community.
A user shares a link to a confidential fraud review file.
A user discusses an active incident in a public employee community.

bankingActivityThemes: AI misuse or external AI upload

riskNotes: May access highly confidential executive and financial files

typicalActivities: Review DLP and insider risk signals
riskNotes: Privileged security role with broad visibility into sensitive telemetry
May export customer-level data if controls are weak
May handle complaint records containing account-like identifiers
May accidentally include internal pricing or risk commentary
Handles privileged legal and regulatory content
May access broad operations data and customer process metrics
Handles compensation, employee records, and HR-sensitive content
Security investigation role with access to sensitive evidence
May access technical logs and device telemetry
May handle customer-level predictions and feature matrices
May expose internal project risks or budget notes if sharing externally
Primary risky-behavior persona for unsafe sharing, excessive downloads, label removal, external AI use, endpoint copy, and ambiguous insider risk scenarios
